actions.text: read through the Fenwick case file one more time before heading out
pull up the missing mammal's last known location on your phone
Morning, Captain. I have a lead on the Fenwick case — can I get a partner assignment?
stop by Nick's desk to loop him in before leaving the precinct
Nick, I need your street contacts. The missing mammal was last seen in Tundratown.
Nick slides on his sunglasses with the expression of someone who already knows this is going to be complicated
take the transit tube toward Tundratown, reviewing witness statements on the way
step off the transit platform into the cold of Tundratown and get your bearings
canvass the street where the mammal was last sighted the night before
Excuse me — Officer Hopps, ZPD. Did you see this mammal here two nights ago?
A polar bear in an expensive suit watches from a fur shop doorway without saying anything
approach the polar bear and hold up your badge
I'm investigating a missing persons case. Your cooperation is appreciated.
note the fur shop address and move on to the next potential witness
Nick reappears from a side alley looking extremely pleased with himself
What did you find?
follow Nick around the corner, out of earshot from the street
Nick produces a crumpled receipt — the missing mammal's name, a date two nights ago, and an address in Little Rodentia
cross-reference the receipt against the case timeline in your notebook
Little Rodentia. Why would a wolf be in Little Rodentia at that hour?
take the connecting transit to the Little Rodentia entrance
navigate carefully through the mouse-scale streets without knocking anything over
A tiny mouse in a chef's apron waves frantically from a doorway — the witness Judy has been looking for
Sir, Officer Hopps, ZPD. I understand you saw something the night of the third?
crouch down to the mouse's eye level and listen carefully
The mouse describes a large mammal being led through the alley, moving like it didn't want to go
ask for the exact alley and mark the location on your map
move toward the alley with Nick close behind
The alley dead-ends at a tunnel entrance — too small for large mammals, exactly right for smuggling
radio your location back to the precinct before entering the tunnel
